steps.title: Apply the zero-product property to the numerator
steps.content: A fraction equals zero when its numerator is zero and its denominator is nonzero. The numerator is $(x-3)(x-8)$. Since it's already factored, setting it equal to zero gives the solutions $$x=3 \quad \text{or} \quad x=8$$

steps.title: Verify solutions and sum them
steps.content:
Check that neither solution makes the denominator zero.
When $x=3$: $3-2=1\neq 0$ ✓
When $x=8$: $8-2=6\neq 0$ ✓
Both solutions are valid. Their sum is $$3+8=11$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
